title: The Pianist
category: European and American Painting, Sculpture, and Decorative Arts
description: Popova’s artistic practice was formed by her extensive travels through Russia and Europe. Here the influence of Cubism and Futurism create a dynamic fragmentation of the human figure. This accomplished construction of intersecting curved and rectilinear planes reflects her interest in form and movement and her affinity for the work of her contemporary, Vladimir Tatlin. Further drama is created by the interplay of black-and-white tones and the texturally rich surface that includes sand and the illusion of wood.
